differential_diagnoses: Ectopic variceal bleeding (secondary to portal hypertension/portal vein stenosis)
Hemobilia due to iatrogenic injury or stricture from prior hepaticojejunostomy
Portal biliopathy with associated vascular complications
Dieulafoy lesion of the stomach or small bowel
next_steps: Interventional radiology consultation for diagnostic and therapeutic portal venography with pressure measurements
Balloon-occluded retrograde transvenous obliteration (BRTO) or variceal embolization if ectopic varices are identified
Selective mesenteric angiography to evaluate for obscure vascular sources of bleeding
Enteroscopy (double-balloon or spiral) with focused inspection of the small bowel and biliary-enteric anastomosis
Liver biopsy or brush cytology if imaging continues to suggest malignancy
Continue supportive care with blood transfusions and iron optimization

differential_diagnoses: Ectopic variceal bleeding (likely at the hepaticojejunostomy site)
Hemobilia due to portal biliopathy and varices within the biliary tree
Portal vein stenosis-induced portal hypertension with underlying gastric/enteric varices
Pseudoaneurysm at the prior hepaticojejunostomy surgical site
Arteriovenous fistula involving the hepatic vasculature
next_steps: Consultation with interventional radiology for diagnostic portal venography with pressure gradient measurement
Direct visualization of the biliary-enteric anastomosis using peroral cholangioscopy or balloon-assisted enteroscopy
Expert radiologic review of the perihilar region specifically evaluating for a pseudoaneurysm
Endoscopic ultrasound with fine-needle aspiration or brush cytology of the perihilar soft tissue mass to rule out malignancy
Consultation with a hepatobiliary surgeon to discuss the feasibility of correcting portal venous outflow obstruction
